en: And again Jesus spoke to them in parables, saying,
zh: 耶穌又用比喻對他們說：

en: "The kingdom of heaven may be compared to a king who gave a wedding feast for his son,
zh: “天國好像一個王，為兒子擺設婚筵。

en: and sent his servants to call those who were invited to the wedding feast, but they would not come.
zh: 他派僕人去叫被邀請的人來參加婚筵。但他們不肯來。

en: Again he sent other servants, saying, 'Tell those who are invited, See, I have prepared my dinner, my oxen and my fat calves have been slaughtered, and everything is ready. Come to the wedding feast.'
zh: 他再派另一些僕人去，說：‘你們告訴被邀請的人，我已經預備好了筵席，公牛和肥畜已經宰了，一切都預備妥當。來參加婚筵吧！’

en: But they paid no attention and went off, one to his farm, another to his business,
zh: 但那些人卻不理會就走了；有的去耕田，有的去作買賣，

en: while the rest seized his servants, treated them shamefully, and killed them.
zh: 其餘的抓住王的僕人，凌辱他們，並且把他們殺了。

en: The king was angry, and he sent his troops and destroyed those murderers and burned their city.
zh: 王就發怒，派兵消滅那些兇手，焚毀他們的城。

en: Then he said to his servants, 'The wedding feast is ready, but those invited were not worthy.
zh: 然後對僕人說：‘婚筵已經預備好了，只是被邀請的人不配。

en: Go therefore to the main roads and invite to the wedding feast as many as you find.'
zh: 所以你們要到大路口，凡遇見的，都請來參加婚筵。’

en: And those servants went out into the roads and gathered all whom they found, both bad and good. So the wedding hall was filled with guests.
zh: “那些僕人就走到街上，把所有遇見的，不論好人壞人，都招聚了來，婚筵上就坐滿了人。

en: "But when the king came in to look at the guests, he saw there a man who had no wedding garment.
zh: 王進來與赴筵的人見面，看見有一個人沒有穿著婚筵的禮服，

en: And he said to him, 'Friend, how did you get in here without a wedding garment?' And he was speechless.
zh: 就對他說：‘朋友，你沒有婚筵的禮服，怎能進到這裡來呢？’他就無話可說。

en: Then the king said to the attendants, 'Bind him hand and foot and cast him into the outer darkness. In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.'
zh: 於是王對侍從說：‘把他的手和腳都綁起來，丟到外面的黑暗裡，在那裡必要哀哭切齒。’

en: For many are called, but few are chosen."
zh: 因為被召的人多，選上的人少。”

en: Then the Pharisees went and plotted how to entangle him in his talk.
zh: 法利賽人就去商量，怎樣找耶穌的話柄來陷害他。

en: And they sent their disciples to him, along with the Herodians, saying, "Teacher, we know that you are true and teach the way of God truthfully, and you do not care about anyone's opinion, for you are not swayed by appearances.
zh: 他們派了自己的門徒和希律黨的人一同去問耶穌：“老師，我們知道你為人誠實，照著真理把 神的道教導人，不顧忌任何人，因為你不徇情面。

en: Tell us, then, what you think. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?"
zh: 請把你的意見告訴我們，納稅給凱撒，可不可以呢？”

en: But Jesus, aware of their malice, said, "Why put me to the test, you hypocrites?
zh: 耶穌看出他們的惡意，就說：“虛偽的人，為甚麼試探我呢？

en: Show me the coin for the tax." And they brought him a denarius.
zh: 拿納稅的錢幣給我看看。”他們就拿了一個銀幣給他。

en: And Jesus said to them, "Whose likeness and inscription is this?"
zh: 耶穌問他們：“這是誰的像，誰的名號？”

en: They said, "Caesar's." Then he said to them, "Therefore render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's."
zh: 他們回答：“凱撒的。”他就對他們說：“凱撒的應當歸給凱撒， 神的應當歸給 神。”

en: When they heard it, they marveled. And they left him and went away.
zh: 他們聽了，十分驚奇，就離開他走了。

en: The same day Sadducees came to him, who say that there is no resurrection, and they asked him a question,
zh: 撒都該人向來認為沒有復活的事。那一天，他們前來問耶穌：

en: saying, "Teacher, Moses said, 'If a man dies having no children, his brother must marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.'
zh: “老師，摩西說：‘如果一個人死了，沒有兒女，他的弟弟應該娶他的妻子，為哥哥立後。’

en: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first married and died, and having no children left his wife to his brother.
zh: 從前我們這裡有兄弟七人，頭一個結了婚，沒有孩子就死了，留下妻子給他的弟弟。

en: So too the second and third, down to the seventh.
zh: 第二個、第三個直到第七個都是這樣。

en: After them all, the woman died.
zh: 最後，那女人也死了。

en: In the resurrection, therefore, of the seven, whose wife will she be? For they all had her."
zh: 那麼，復活的時候，她是這七個人中哪一個的妻子呢？因為他們都娶過她。”

en: But Jesus answered them, "You are wrong, because you know neither the Scriptures nor the power of God.
zh: 耶穌回答他們：“你們錯了，因為你們不明白聖經，也不曉得 神的能力。

en: For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
zh: 復活的時候，人們也不娶也不嫁，而是像天上的使者一樣。

en: And as for the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what was said to you by God:
zh: 關於死人復活的事， 神對你們講過：‘我是亞伯拉罕的 神，以撒的 神，雅各的 神’，你們沒有念過嗎？ 神不是死人的 神，而是活人的 神。”

en: 'I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'? He is not God of the dead, but of the living."
And when the crowd heard it, they were astonished at his teaching.
zh: 群眾聽了他的教訓，就十分詫異。

en: But when the Pharisees heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together.
zh: 法利賽人聽見耶穌使撒都該人無話可說，就聚集在一起。

en: And one of them, a lawyer, asked him a question to test him.
zh: 他們中間有一個律法家，試探耶穌說：

en: "Teacher, which is the great commandment in the Law?"
zh: “老師，律法中哪一條誡命是最重要的呢？”

en: And he said to him, "You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind.
zh: 他回答：“你要全心、全性、全意愛主你的 神。

en: This is the great and first commandment.
zh: 這是最重要的第一條誡命。

en: And a second is like it: You shall love your neighbor as yourself.
zh: 第二條也和它相似，就是要愛人如己。

en: On these two commandments depend all the Law and the Prophets."
zh: 全部律法和先知書，都以這兩條誡命作為根據。”

en: Now while the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them a question,
zh: 法利賽人聚在一起的時候，耶穌問他們：

en: saying, "What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he?" They said to him, "The son of David."
zh: “你們對基督的看法怎樣？他是誰的子孫呢？”他們回答：“大衛的子孫。”

en: He said to them, "How is it then that David, in the Spirit, calls him Lord, saying,
zh: 耶穌就說：“那麼大衛被聖靈感動，怎麼會稱他為主呢？他說：

en: "'The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet'?
zh: ‘主對我的主說： 你坐在我的右邊， 等我把你的仇敵放在你的腳下。’

en: If then David calls him Lord, how is he his son?"
zh: “大衛既然稱他為主，他怎麼又是大衛的子孫呢？”

en: And no one was able to answer him a word, nor from that day did anyone dare to ask him any more questions.
zh: 沒有人能夠回答他。從那天起，也沒有人敢再問他。
